title: odontites luteus subsp. luteus
intro: Odontites luteus subsp. luteus, also known as Yellow Toothwort or Yellow Odontites, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Orobanchaceae, which is a group of flowering plants known as broomrapes or toothworts. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants, typically grasses and herbs. The medicinal properties of Odontites luteus subsp. luteus include its diuretic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat urinary disorders and reduce inflammation. The plant's health benefits include its ability to promote digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and support the immune system, and it can be prepared as a tea or infusion, or used in herbal tinctures.
